terms.meaning_summary: 1) to be hot, furious, burn, becomeangry, be kindled 1a) (Qal) to burn, kindle (anger) 1b) (Niphal) to be angry with, be incensed 1c) (Hiphil) to burn, kindle
1) to bind, besiege, confine, cramp 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to confine, secure 1a2) to shut in, beseige
1) to bind, be narrow, be in distress, make narrow, cause distress, besiege, be straitened, be bound 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to bind, tie up, shut up 1a2) to be scant, be cramped, be in straits
1) to disturb, alarm, terrify, hurry, be disturbed, be anxious, be afraid, be hurried, be nervous 1a) (Niphal) 1a1) to be disturbed, dismayed, terrified, anxious 1a2) to be in haste, be hasty
to suffer distress

(act.) to cause sorrow, grief; (pass.) to be sorrowful, sad, distressed grieveto occasion grief, or sorrow to, to distress, 2Cor. 2:2, 5; 7:8; passive to be grieved, pained, distressed, sorrowful, Mt. 17:23; 19:22; to aggrieve, cross, vex, Eph. 4:30; passive to feel pained, Rom. 14:15
1) to be hard, be severe, be fierce, be harsh 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to be hard, be difficult 1a2) to be hard, be severe
to cover (ears); to crowd (against); to guard, hold in custody; to compel, urge on;
1) to hurt, pain, grieve, displease, vex, wrest 1a) (Qal) to hurt, pain 1b)(Niphal) to be in pain, be pained, be grieved 1c) (Piel) to vex, torture
1) to be grieved, loathe, abhor, feel a loathing or abhorrence or sickening dread 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to feel a loathing at, abhor 1a2) to feel a sickening dread
1) to put forth, mediate, muse, commune, speak, complain, ponder, sing 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to complain 1a2) to muse, meditate upon, study, ponder
(passive) to be oppressed, distressed oppress to exhaust by labor, or suffering; to wear out, distress 2Pet. 2:7; to overpower, oppress, Acts 7:24* LSJ dictionary
1) to press, drive, oppress, exact, exert demanding pressure 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to press, drive 1a2) to exact
1) to be in pain, be sore, have pain, be sorrowful 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to be in pain (physical) 1a2) to be in pain (mental)
(Hiphil) to constrain, press, bring into straits, straiten, oppress
(act.) to press upon, crowd up to; cause trouble; (passive) to be narrow; to be pressed, troubled, persecuted

(passive) to be crushed; to withhold, be restricted press to crowd together into a narrow place, straiten; , passive metaphorically to be in straits, to be cooped up, to be cramped from action, 2Cor. 4:8; to be cramped, restrict in feeling, 2Cor. 6:12*
(Qal) to show hostility to, be an adversary, treat as foe
to make a rival-wife
to bother, annoy; (passive) to be harassed; (middle) to trouble oneself to flay, lacerate;
: tremble/fear 1) to twist, whirl, dance, writhe, fear, tremble, travail, be in anguish, be pained 1a) (Qal) 1a1) to dance

verse_records.verse_text: but for Cain and his offering he had no regard. So Cain was very angry, and his face fell.
The Lord said to Cain, “Why are you angry, and why has your face fallen?
Then he said, “Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak. Suppose thirty are found there.” He answered, “I will not do it, if I find thirty there.”
Then he said, “Oh let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak again but this once. Suppose ten are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of ten I will not destroy it.”

and my wrath will burn, and I will kill you with the sword, and your wives shall become widows and your children fatherless.
